name: Alabama
id_requirements: Alabama photo ID required at polls
Valid options: Driver's license, State ID, US Passport, Military ID, Tribal ID
mail_in_steps: Request absentee ballot at least 5 days before election
notes: No early voting. Photo ID strictly required.

name: Alaska
id_requirements: Non-photo ID accepted
Options: Voter ID card, utility bill, bank statement, government document
mail_in_steps: Request absentee ballot online or by mail
Must be received by Election Day
notes: Alaska uses ranked-choice voting.

name: Arizona
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license, AZ state ID, passport, or tribal ID
Non-photo ID with two proofs of address also accepted
mail_in_steps: Arizona automatically mails ballots to Active Early Voter List members
notes: Permanent early voter list available.

name: Arkansas
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license, state ID, US passport, military ID, or concealed carry license
mail_in_steps: Request at least 7 days before election

name: California
id_requirements: No photo ID required for registered voters
First-time voters may need ID
mail_in_steps: All registered voters receive a mail ballot automatically
Return by mail (postmarked by Election Day) or drop box by Election Day
notes: Vote-by-mail is default for all voters.

name: Colorado
id_requirements: ID required for first-time voters
Photo or non-photo ID accepted
mail_in_steps: All active voters receive mail ballots automatically
Sign the affidavit on the envelope
notes: Universal mail voting state.

name: Connecticut
id_requirements: ID required for first-time voters only
Non-photo ID accepted (utility bill, bank statement)
mail_in_steps: No excuse required for absentee ballot
Request online, by mail, or in person
notes: Early voting expanded in 2024.

name: Delaware
id_requirements: Photo ID not required for registered voters
Signature verification used
mail_in_steps: Request at least 7 days before election

name: Florida
id_requirements: Photo and signature ID required
Driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID, concealed weapons license
notes: Special Senate election also on ballot.

name: Georgia
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID, tribal ID, or student ID from GA public college
notes: Georgia requires photo ID for absentee ballots.

name: Hawaii
id_requirements: No ID required for registered voters
Signature used for verification
mail_in_steps: All registered voters automatically receive mail ballots
notes: Universal vote-by-mail state.

name: Idaho
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license, state ID, passport, student ID, military ID, or tribal ID
mail_in_steps: No excuse required for absentee ballot

name: Illinois
id_requirements: ID not required for registered voters
First-time voters may need documentation
mail_in_steps: No excuse required for vote by mail
notes: One of the earliest primary dates.

name: Indiana
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID
mail_in_steps: Valid excuses: illness, disability, travel on Election Day

name: Iowa
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Iowa voter ID card, driver's license, non-driver ID, passport, military ID, or student ID
mail_in_steps: No excuse required for absentee ballot

name: Kansas
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID, concealed carry license
mail_in_steps: No excuse required for advance mail ballot

name: Kentucky
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID, or employee ID
notes: Limited early voting window.

name: Louisiana
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Louisiana driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID, or student ID with address
mail_in_steps: Valid excuses: illness, disability, work hours, religious observance
notes: Louisiana uses a jungle primary on Election Day itself.

name: Maine
id_requirements: No ID required for registered voters
First-time voters may need documentation
notes: Uses ranked-choice voting for federal races.

name: Maryland
id_requirements: ID required for first-time voters only
Driver's license, utility bill, bank statement, government check
mail_in_steps: Ballots automatically mailed to all active voters

name: Massachusetts
id_requirements: No photo ID required for registered voters
First-time voters need name/address verification
mail_in_steps: All voters may request mail ballot
Return by 8pm Election Day (postmark by Election Day)

name: Michigan
id_requirements: Photo ID preferred
Without photo ID, sign an affidavit — ballot still counted
mail_in_steps: No excuse required for absentee ballot
All voters have permanent status rights

name: Minnesota
id_requirements: Photo ID not required
Vouching system accepted — neighbor can vouch for you
notes: Longest early voting window in the country.

name: Mississippi
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID, government employee ID, firearms license, concealed carry permit
mail_in_steps: Valid excuses: illness, disability, religious observance, travel
notes: No early voting. Strict absentee requirements.

name: Missouri
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID, or student ID

name: Montana
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID, student ID, tribal ID
notes: Same-day registration available.

name: Nebraska
id_requirements: Photo ID required since 2023
Driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID, student ID
mail_in_steps: No excuse required for early voting by mail

name: Nevada
id_requirements: ID required for first-time voters
Photo or non-photo ID accepted
mail_in_steps: All active voters automatically receive mail ballots
notes: Universal vote-by-mail state.

name: New Hampshire
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID, student ID
mail_in_steps: Valid excuses: absence from state, illness, disability, religious observance
notes: No early voting. Same-day registration available.

name: New Jersey
id_requirements: ID required for first-time voters
Driver's license, utility bill, bank statement, or government document
mail_in_steps: All registered voters automatically receive mail ballots
notes: Universal vote-by-mail since 2020.

name: New Mexico
id_requirements: No photo ID required for registered voters
Last 4 digits of SSN or NM driver's license may be needed to verify identity

name: New York
id_requirements: No photo ID required
First-time voters need name/address documentation
mail_in_steps: Request at least 7 days before election
Return by 9pm Election Day or postmark by Election Day

name: North Carolina
id_requirements: Photo ID required since 2023
Driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID, student ID, tribal ID, or voter ID card
notes: Early primary date — check registration by February 6.

name: North Dakota
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license or state ID with current address required. No PO Box.
mail_in_steps: No excuse required for absentee ballot
notes: North Dakota does NOT require voter registration.

name: Ohio
id_requirements: Photo ID required since 2023
Driver's license, state ID, military ID, or Ohio photo ID card
mail_in_steps: No excuse required for absentee ballot
notes: Special Senate election on ballot.

name: Oklahoma
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID
mail_in_steps: Valid excuses: illness, disability, age 65+, religious observance, absence from county
notes: Very limited early voting window.

name: Oregon
id_requirements: No ID required at polls (vote by mail only)
First-time voters: signature verification
mail_in_steps: All registered voters automatically receive mail ballots
Sign the return envelope — signature is ID
Return by 8pm Election Day or drop at drop box
notes: Oregon is exclusively vote-by-mail since 1998.

name: Pennsylvania
id_requirements: Photo ID required for first-time voters at their polling place
Driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID, or student ID
mail_in_steps: No excuse required for mail-in ballot
Return by 8pm Election Day — must be in inner and outer envelope correctly
notes: Important: Ballots must be in inner secrecy envelope inside outer envelope.

name: Rhode Island
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID, or student ID

name: South Carolina
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID, or SC voter registration card with photo
mail_in_steps: Valid excuses: illness, disability, employment, travel

name: South Dakota
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID, tribal ID
mail_in_steps: No excuse required for absentee ballot
Request by the day before election
Return by close of polls on Election Day

name: Tennessee
id_requirements: Photo ID required (strict)
TN driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID, or US government photo ID
mail_in_steps: Valid excuses: illness, disability, absence from county, hospitalization, caretaker
Return by close of polls on Election Day

name: Texas
id_requirements: Photo ID required (strict)
TX driver's license, TX state ID, US passport, military ID, Texas handgun license, citizenship certificate with photo
mail_in_steps: Valid only for: age 65+, illness/disability, absence from county, jail with pending trial
notes: One of the strictest ID and absentee rules. Register by October 4 for November election.

name: Utah
id_requirements: No ID required for registered voters
Signature verification for mail ballots
mail_in_steps: All registered voters automatically receive mail ballots
Sign the affidavit on the return envelope
notes: Universal vote-by-mail state.

name: Vermont
id_requirements: No ID required
Self-certification of eligibility suffices
mail_in_steps: All registered voters receive mail ballots automatically
notes: Easiest voting access in the country.

name: Virginia
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID, student ID, or Virginia Voter ID
mail_in_steps: Request by 5pm the Friday before the election
notes: Extended early voting window.

name: Washington
id_requirements: No ID required at polls (vote by mail only)
Signature on ballot is ID
mail_in_steps: All registered voters automatically receive mail ballots
Return by 8pm Election Day (postmark by Election Day)
notes: Washington is exclusively vote-by-mail.

name: West Virginia
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license, state ID, military ID, student ID, employee ID, or bank card with photo
mail_in_steps: No excuse required for absentee ballot

name: Wisconsin
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Wisconsin driver's license, state ID, military ID, passport, tribal ID, or student ID (must be recently issued)
mail_in_steps: No excuse required for absentee ballot
Return by 8pm Election Day — must be witnessed and signed
notes: Absentee ballot requires a witness signature.

name: Wyoming
id_requirements: Photo ID required
Driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID, or student ID
mail_in_steps: No excuse required for absentee ballot

name: Washington D.C.
id_requirements: No photo ID required for registered voters
First-time registrants need proof of residency
mail_in_steps: All registered voters automatically receive mail ballots
notes: DC residents can vote representative and senators but have no voting representation in Congress.
